evals.prompt: Write a Python service class that handles user registration: it should validate the email format, save the user to a PostgreSQL database, and send a welcome email. The user has a name, email, and password.
evals.expected_output: Separate classes for domain model, persistence, and notification. Dependencies injected via constructor typed as Protocol. No raw SQL mixed with domain logic. No speculative parameters.

assertions.name: protocol_or_abc_for_dependencies
assertions.description: Dependencies (db, mailer) are defined as Protocol or ABC, not concrete classes

assertions.name: constructor_injection
assertions.description: Service class receives dependencies via __init__ params, not instantiated inline
assertions.check: no 'psycopg2.connect' or 'smtplib.SMTP' inside the main service class body

assertions.name: srp_domain_separated_from_persistence
assertions.description: Domain model class exists separately from the persistence/repository class
assertions.check: at least two distinct classes: one for domain data, one for persistence

assertions.name: srp_notification_separated
assertions.description: No single class handles both persistence AND email notification
assertions.check: email/mailer logic is in a separate class from the db/repository logic

assertions.name: type_annotations_on_all_methods
assertions.description: All function and method signatures include type annotations
assertions.check: all def statements have parameter types and return type annotations

evals.prompt:
Refactor this Python class to improve its design:

```python
class OrderManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.db = psycopg2.connect('postgresql://localhost/orders')
        self.smtp = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com')

    def process_order(self, order_id: int, user_id: int) -> dict:
        cursor = self.db.cursor()
        cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = %s', (order_id,))
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        if row is None:
            raise ValueError('Order not found')
        order_data = {'id': row[0], 'total': row[1], 'status': row[2]}
        if order_data['status'] == 'pending' and order_data['total'] > 0:
            order_data['status'] = 'confirmed'
            cursor.execute('UPDATE orders SET status = %s WHERE id = %s', ('confirmed', order_id))
            self.db.commit()
            cursor.execute('SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = %s', (user_id,))
            user_row = cursor.fetchone()
            msg = MIMEText(f'Your order {order_id} is confirmed!')
            msg['To'] = user_row[0]
            self.smtp.send_message(msg)
        return order_data
```
evals.expected_output: SRP applied: split into Order domain model, OrderRepository, OrderNotifier. Tell-Don't-Ask: order.confirm() instead of external state mutation. DIP: dependencies as Protocol types injected via constructor. SLA: process_order body at one abstraction level. LoD: no chained attribute access.

assertions.name: tell_dont_ask_state_transition
assertions.description: State transition encapsulated in the domain object (e.g. order.confirm()), not externally mutated
assertions.check: no pattern 'order_data["status"] = ' or 'order.status = ' in the orchestrating service

assertions.name: no_raw_sql_in_service
assertions.description: The orchestrating service/processor no longer contains raw SQL strings
assertions.check: no 'cursor.execute' or 'SELECT' string in the main service method

assertions.name: dip_injected_deps
assertions.description: DB and SMTP connections are injected, not instantiated in __init__
assertions.check: no 'psycopg2.connect' or 'smtplib.SMTP' in any class __init__ body

assertions.name: srp_repository_exists
assertions.description: A dedicated Repository or DAO class exists for order persistence
assertions.check: file contains a class named with 'Repository', 'Repo', or 'DAO'

assertions.name: sla_uniform_abstraction
assertions.description: The main processing method body is at one abstraction level — domain calls only, no SQL
assertions.check: process_order (or equivalent) body contains only domain-level calls

evals.prompt: I need to add a payment processing feature to my Python app. It should support Stripe, PayPal, and bank transfer. More payment providers might be added in the future.
evals.expected_output: PaymentProvider Protocol (ISP: minimal interface). Strategy dict dispatch (OCP: extend without modifying). No if/elif provider chain. YAGNI respected — no speculative abstractions beyond the three stated providers. Constructor injection for the processor.

assertions.name: ocp_no_if_elif_provider_chain
assertions.description: No if/elif chain switching on provider type string in the main processing logic
assertions.check: no 'if.*stripe' / 'elif.*paypal' style chain in the charge/process method

assertions.name: protocol_interface_defined
assertions.description: A PaymentProvider Protocol or ABC is defined as the abstraction boundary
assertions.check: file contains 'Protocol' or 'ABC' for payment interface

assertions.name: strategy_registry_or_dispatch
assertions.description: A dict, registry, or singledispatch maps provider names to implementations
assertions.check: file contains a dict mapping provider keys to strategy instances or classes

assertions.name: isp_minimal_interface
assertions.description: The payment Protocol/ABC has a focused minimal interface (1-3 methods)
assertions.check: Protocol class has no more than 3 abstract methods

assertions.name: yagni_no_speculative_plugin_system
assertions.description: No over-engineered plugin loader, auto-discovery, or abstract factory for hypothetical providers
